meta.name: pcf-dependent-libraries
meta.title: Pcf Dependent Libraries Skill
meta.description: Using dependent libraries in PCF components Triggers on: **/*.{ts,tsx,js,json,xml,pcfproj,csproj}

original_definition.skill_description: Using dependent libraries in PCF components Triggers on: **/*.{ts,tsx,js,json,xml,pcfproj,csproj}

actions.name: With model-driven apps, you can reuse a prebuilt library con
actions.description: With model-driven apps, you can reuse a prebuilt library contained in another component that is loaded as a dependency to more than one component.

actions.name: Having copies of a prebuilt library in multiple controls is
actions.description: Having copies of a prebuilt library in multiple controls is undesirable. Reusing existing libraries improves performance, especially when the library

actions.name: 1. Create a Library component that contains the library. Thi
actions.description: 1. Create a Library component that contains the library. This component can provide some functionality or only be a container for the library.

actions.name: 2. Configure another component to depend on the library load
actions.description: 2. Configure another component to depend on the library loaded by the library component.

actions.name: By default, the library loads when the dependent component l
actions.description: By default, the library loads when the dependent component loads, but you can configure it to load on demand.

actions.name: You need to add configuration data to your component project
actions.description: You need to add configuration data to your component project so that the build process deploys your libraries the way you want. Set this configuration

actions.name: Edit the manifest schema to Register dependencies
actions.description: Edit the manifest schema to Register dependencies

actions.name: Add this file to override the default feature flags for a co
actions.description: Add this file to override the default feature flags for a component without modifying the files generated in the `node_modules` folder.

actions.name: The build process for components uses [Webpack](https://webp
actions.description: The build process for components uses [Webpack](https://webpack.js.org/) to bundle the code and dependencies into a deployable asset. To exclude your
